title: Adverse Event Investigation Routing
description: A hospital-operations and compliance workflow that enforces control checks while allocating operational resources and closing governance loops. Focus case: adverse event investigation routing.
assumptions: Investigators, technicians, and resources are represented as discrete, trackable entities whose availability is updated when allocated or released.
Predefined compatibility relations (e.g., eligibility matrices) exist to indicate which investigators, panels, departments, and templates are appropriate for each case.
Evidence kits and notification channels are consumable resources that must be reserved before use and released after completion.
Regulatory templates and external agency mappings are available and maintained so that report-generation actions can be performed deterministically.
Closure requires completion of specified review, remediation, and documentation predicates to ensure governance sign-off.
risks: Delays in assignment or resource availability may stall investigations and violate regulatory timelines.
Misclassification of departmental or specialty eligibility could route cases to inappropriate teams, degrading analysis quality.
Concurrent allocation conflicts for consumable resources (evidence kits, channels) may cause failed collection or lost chain-of-custody.
Insufficient documentation or missing template associations could prevent successful external reporting or regulatory compliance.
Overreliance on a single oversight entity may bottleneck approvals, increasing time to closure.
Potential privacy or data-handling lapses during evidence collection and storage if secure buckets are not enforced.
